content.text: The Agent Communication Protocol (ACP), developed by IBM BeeAI, is an open REST + SSE standard for invoking agents. Where A2A centres on Tasks and AgentCards, ACP organises work around

— discrete invocations of an agent — and uses a multipart message format that accommodates text, files, and structured data in the same payload. ACP is designed to be OpenAPI-compatible so standard HTTP tooling (API gateways, auto-generated clients, Swagger UIs) works out of the box.

attrs.title: ACP Run states: created → in_progress → completed / awaiting_input / failed

A2A vs ACP — Choosing Between Them
When an ACP agent needs a human decision it transitions the Run to
and includes a prompt message in the event. The client resumes the Run by posting to

that group related Runs, similar to a conversation thread. A Session retains context across multiple Runs so the agent can refer back to earlier exchanges without re-sending the full history on every call:
A Run that fails returns a
object containing a machine-readable code and a human-readable message. Long-running Runs can be cancelled by the client with
The cleanest integration point is wrapping your LangGraph graph behind the ACP server endpoints. The graph executes inside
while FastAPI handles the HTTP + SSE protocol:
; each Run is stateful, queryable, and resumable.
Multipart Messages with typed Parts accommodate text, JSON, and binary content in the same payload — no custom serialisation needed.
re-enter the agent without re-running from scratch.
Prefer ACP over A2A when OpenAPI compatibility is a hard requirement; prefer A2A when deploying on Google Cloud or Vertex AI.
